free_text: Low-lying states in the unbound N-11 nucleus.
University of Connecticut, Storrs, USA
S B
Last checking has been done.
*By authors*.The method of scattering of protons in
inverse kinematics has been used to identify low-lying
resonances in the proton unbound nucleus N-11.
The C-10 atoms were produced through the B-10(p,n)C-10
reaction by bombarding a natural boron nitride target
with an intense 30-MeV proton beam.
A 3-mg/cm**2 polyethylene-(CH(2))N-foil was used. The
target thickness was chosen such that the expected full
width of the 1/2+ ground state could be encompassed
at a single beam energy, while limiting the total
energy straggling of the beam in the target.
.The R-matrix framework.
For
calibration the current amplifier at the Faraday cup.
The recoil protons were detected using a
compact dick silicon strip detector based delta-E-E
telescope array called Cd-PAD, which allows for very
clean separation between proton,alpha and beta-
particles. The Cd-PAD array was situated 12.1-cm down-
stream from the target. The energy calibration of the
detector array was performed by means of a three-line
alpha-source-Pu-239, Am-241, cm-244. The laboratory
proton energy resolution was 55-keV for the delta-E
detectors and 90-keV for the PAD-detectors.
.The error in the number of counts for the solid
angles of the detectors is 5%.
. The error in the H-content in target is 10%.
.The error in the total number of incident beam
particles is 15%.
Data were received from C.Angulo
.The uncertainty is reported by authors.
